user_query: I'm a working session guitarist and want my small static site to rank and read better without over-claiming. Please optimize the three HTML pages in site/pages using the brand voice in site/site_config.yaml, and produce updated files and a verification report. Requirements: 1) Read local inputs: - Config: site/site_config.yaml (use base_url for sitemap/robots and honor brand_voice in copy). - Pages: site/pages/index.html, site/pages/lessons.html, site/pages/setup.html (each includes an HTML comment with lastmod: YYYY-MM-DD). 2) Rewrite and apply on-page SEO for each HTML page: - Extract the H1 and lead paragraph to understand intent and tone. - Propose and insert a revised (<= 60 characters) and a revised <meta name="description"> (length between 150–160 characters inclusive) that are clear, respectful, concise, and reflect the brand_voice. Avoid superlatives or over-selling (e.g., no "best", "world-class"). - Include an appropriate focus phrase per page, used once if natural: • index.html: include "session guitarist" • lessons.html: include "private guitar lessons" • setup.html: include "guitar setup" - Mention "Maestro Rivera" at most once in each meta description and only if it reads naturally. - Do not change visible body copy beyond adding image alt text (see below). Preserve existing structure. 3) Image alt text: - For every <img> tag on each page, add or replace the alt attribute with a concise, descriptive noun phrase (<= 100 characters) derived from the nearest figcaption or nearby text. Avoid keyword stuffing and keep it human-friendly. 4) Outputs: - Write updated HTML files to output/pages/ with the same basenames (e.g., site/pages/index.html -> output/pages/index.html). Ensure the new <title>, <meta name="description">, and alt attributes are present in these outputs. - Generate output/seo/sitemap.xml that lists all three pages using absolute URLs built from base_url in site/site_config.yaml plus the filenames. Use the lastmod date from each page’s HTML comment. Set changefreq to weekly. Set priority to 0.8 for index.html and 0.6 for the others. - Generate output/seo/robots.txt that allows all crawling and includes a Sitemap directive pointing to {base_url}/sitemap.xml (use the same base_url value from the YAML). - Create output/reports/seo_changes.json summarizing, for each page: source and output paths; old_title and new_title; old_meta_description (empty if missing) and new_meta_description; title_length and description_length; constraint checks (title_len_ok, description_len_ok, focus_phrase_included, maestro_mentioned_once_or_less); and images_updated array with objects {src, old_alt (null if missing or placeholder), new_alt}. Validation notes: - The exact strings for new titles, meta descriptions, and alt text must appear in the corresponding output/pages/*.html files. - sitemap.xml must use the base_url from the YAML and the lastmod dates from the HTML comments. - robots.txt must include a line: Sitemap: {base_url}/sitemap.xml Deliverables: - Updated pages: output/pages/index.html, output/pages/lessons.html, output/pages/setup.html - SEO files: output/seo/sitemap.xml, output/seo/robots.txt - Report: output/reports/seo_changes.json Please keep the tone deferential and professional, acknowledging Maestro Rivera where natural, and keep everything deterministic based only on the provided files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
